reasoning: Fundamental forecasts for USD/CNY in Q2 2025 point toward an expected rise to ~7.40 as new US tariffs take effect, suggesting an increase over the current ~7.29 baseline. Additionally, the FRED short-term linear model projects continued upward momentum with an expected value of 7.31 by April 20. However, the People's Bank of China routinely intervenes to smooth volatility, as seen in the recent correction from 7.35 to 7.29, keeping moves contained. Heading into late 2025, a weaker USD is broadly anticipated due to projected Federal Reserve rate cuts, which will counteract some of the upward pressure. Following forecasting principles, the initial medium-term upward bias shrinks toward 0.5 for increasingly distant resolution dates to account for escalating uncertainty over these longer horizons.
